posts.text: Sorry, I didn't mean to suggest that this is a falsehood you've been promoting. "continue" was re: how the miyazaki quote is often invoked. My issue with your post here was in framing this as his response to gen-AI, which I don't think is accurate. If anything, it's his response to half-baked RL.

reply_context.text: David, I don't know why you use the word "continue" since this is my first post on this matter. Furthermore, I don't claim anything you seem to think I claim. Please reread my post ...

posts.text: You don't need to continue promoting the falsehood that his statements here were anti-AI broadly. They were anti this specific project and its disgusting results.

posts.text: I feel like it's unlikely we'll be able to improve on nature. "Direct air capture" will probably be a shorthand for farming rapid growth rate organisms, like algae or bamboo or something like that.

reply_context.text: Direct air capture (DAC) is still such an active area of research that it’s crazy to me that there are startups selling CO₂ removal (CDR) credits based on the method as if we know how to do it, or that it’s even possible.
